name: Open-Face Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 10 Chinese dried black mushrooms, softened in hot water for 20 minutes, stems removed and caps finely chopped
2 cups Minced garlic chives (or substitute 2 cups minced leeks and 1 tablespoon minced garlic)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the filling by chopping the ground pork until fluffy. Mix it with all the remaining ingredients in a bowl.
2. Place a heaping tablespoon of the filling in the middle of each dumpling skin (30 skins).
3. Gather the edges of the skin around the filling, forming a "waist" by squeezing in the center.
4. Push the filling up from the bottom to create a flat surface and smooth the top with the bottom of a spoon dipped in water.
5. Line steamer trays or aluminum pie plates (lightly brushed with sesame oil) with damp cheesecloth or parchment paper brushed with sesame oil.
6. Arrange the shaped dumplings about 1/4-inch apart on the steamers or plates.
7. Fill a wok or spaghetti pot so the bottom edge of the steamer tray is covered by water. Heat until boiling.
8. Stack the steamer trays one on top of the other and place over the boiling water, or fit pie plates in the pot and cover with the lid.
9. Steam for about 15 minutes, reversing stacked trays after 8 minutes.
10. Remove cooked dumplings and steam as necessary. Sprinkle minced scallion greens over the finished dumplings before serving with dipping sauces.
